summary: Completed comprehensive testing of RED RIBBON OPS PORTAL video editing workflow system. Backend APIs 100% functional, frontend 90% functional with one role assignment bug.

ui_bugs.issue: Role assignment bug - Requester users created through UI are assigned Editor role instead of Requester role
ui_bugs.details: When creating a user with 'Requester' role through the admin UI, the user is saved with 'Editor' role instead. This prevents proper testing of Requester workflow.

passed_tests: Admin login with correct credentials (admin@redribbonops.com / admin123)
Admin dashboard loads with 5 KPI cards (Open, In Progress, Pending Review, Delivered, SLA Breaching)
User management page accessible and functional
Editor user creation through admin UI
Editor login and dashboard functionality
Editor dashboard shows proper sections (New Orders pool, In Progress, Sent for Review, etc.)
Navigation between Dashboard, Users, and All Orders pages
Authentication system (login/logout functionality)
Role-based dashboard rendering (Admin vs Editor dashboards)
All 20 backend API endpoints (100% success rate)
Seed data creation (admin user initialization)
User CRUD operations via API
Order creation, picking, and workflow transitions via API
Message thread functionality via API
File management and final delivery marking via API
Dashboard statistics for all roles via API
Notifications system via API
Complete order workflow: Open → In Progress → Pending → Delivered via API
SLA tracking and breach detection
Email notification system (SMTP placeholder working)
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix role assignment bug in user creation form - when admin creates a user with 'Requester' role, the user is incorrectly saved with 'Editor' role. Check the user creation form submission logic and ensure the selected role value is properly passed to the backend API.
